channel.title: Kano Git Master Skill
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Kano Git Master Skill

item.title: API Overview
item.description: API Overview This site publishes generated C++ API documentation under /api-docs/. Open generated API docs The generated API pages are produced from the public C++ facade headers with Doxygen.

item.title: index
item.description: Git Master Skill Documentation Version: 0.0.1 Status: Alpha Release Current command contract updated: 2026-06-22 This documentation set contains both current native-CLI docs ...

item.title: changelog
item.description: Changelog All notable changes to the Git Master Skill project. [Unreleased] Validation Gates Public release candidates must pass git diff --check, the public docs script-reference audit, the pre-commit quality gate, and a native version/help smoke check before tagging.

item.title: ci-cd-trigger-policy
item.description: CI/CD Trigger Policy Date: 2026-06-23 Backlog items: KG-TSK-0093, KG-TSK-0094 Policy Jenkins is the canonical CI/CD runner for kano-git-master-skill. GitHub Actions remains available for public-safe checks, GitHub Pages publishing, and explicitly reviewed/manual release preparation.

item.title: cpp-stage-refactor-backlog
item.description: C++ Stage Refactor Backlog Status: draft local issue set — 6/6 complete ✅ Scope: src/cpp/shared/infra + src/cpp/scripts Why this exists GitHub issue creation is blocked in the current ...

item.title: pgo-coverage-shared-infra-tracking
item.description: PGO and coverage shared-infra tracking Status: tracking note for KG-TSK-0088 Updated: 2026-06-23 Scope This note tracks how kano-git-master-skill currently consumes the shared ...

item.title: git-svn
item.description: Git-SVN Bridge Guide Feature: Subversion integration via git-svn Phase: 7 Status: Complete Overview Git-SVN is a bridge that allows you to use Git as a client for Subversion (SVN) ...

item.title: ignore-datasource-sync-policy
item.description: Ignore Datasource Sync Policy This guide defines how to refresh the upstream ignore template datasource (github/gitignore) in a deterministic and auditable way ...

item.title: ignore-plan-operator-workflow
item.description: Ignore Plan Operator Workflow This guide is for day-to-day operators who run the ignore stage in plan pipelines. It focuses on: deterministic command sequence (init -> ignore-init -> verify -> apply) fast troubleshooting from error text to next action evidence checklist for bug reports and audit trails Scope This guide covers only stages.ignore operations: kog plan new kog plan ignore-init kog plan verify pre-apply --stage ignore kog plan apply --stage ignore kog plan verify ignore --context plan Standard Flow Use one plan file consistently in the whole run: # 1) Create or refresh plan template ./kog plan new --plan-file .kano/tmp/git/plans/default-plan.json --force # 2) Populate ignore candidates from current working tree ./kog plan ignore-init --plan-file .kano/tmp/git/plans/default-plan.json --max-per-repo 200 # 3) Validate ignore-stage schema and required shape ./kog plan verify pre-apply --stage ignore --plan-file .kano/tmp/git/plans/default-plan.json # 4) Apply merged/deduplicated rules to targets ./kog plan apply --stage ignore --plan-file .kano/tmp/git/plans/default-plan.json # 5) Confirm gate status ./kog plan verify ignore --context plan Troubleshooting Matrix Case: plan file not found/readable Typical error: Error: plan file not found/readable: <path> Action: Run kog plan new --plan-file "<path>" --force Re-run the original command with the same --plan-file.

item.title: kcc-commit-message-policy
item.description: KCC Commit Message Policy Kano Commit Convention (KCC) gives agent and human commits a stable subject shape for backlog traceability and reporting. Subject Shape Use: [Area][Intent] Concise subject (TICKET-ID) Examples: [Docs][Feature] Revamp README landing page (KOB-TSK-0042) [KOG][BugFix] Keep plan-file commits intent-scoped (KOA-TSK-0149) [Backlog][Task] Link public artifact contract (KG-FTR-0014) Area examples: KOG Backlog Docs Build Submodule Release Intent examples: Feature BugFix Task Docs Chore Maintenance Ticket IDs should use the backlog identifier that owns the work, such as KOB-TSK-0042, KOA-TSK-0149, or KG-FTR-0014.
